name: Logical Rewriting
guide_hints:
# Level 8: Logical Rewriting (`rw`)

The `rw` command is used to rewrite logically equivalent formulas. If you have a proven definition or equivalence `A ⇔ B`, you can use `rw` to replace occurrences of `A` with `B` (or vice-versa) inside a target formula.

### Syntax
`rw <thm> [occ] [<target>] [<out>] [<equiv>]`
- `<thm>`: the equivalence theorem `A ⇔ B` or definition you want to use.
- `[occ]`: which occurrence to replace (optional, defaults to all).
- `<target>`: the formula to rewrite.
- `<out>`: the name of the new rewritten formula.
Just like other commands, the 0-argument and 1-argument rules apply! If you omit `<target>` and `<out>`, it acts on the current goal formula in-place.

### Instructions:
Create propositional variables `p`, `q`, and `r`.
Create the goal: `cf goal (p ⇔ q) ⇒ (p ∨ r ⇔ q ∨ r)`.
Start a mission: `mission goal`.

You have premise `p ⇔ q`. Your goal is `p ∨ r ⇔ q ∨ r`.
Type `rw <name of premise>`. It will rewrite `p` to `q` in the goal, changing the goal to `q ∨ r ⇔ q ∨ r`!
Now prove this new goal by applying `PC1` or `auto`, and the mission will close!
